caseStudies.pageSubtitle: Real stories from clients using Zonda

hero.title: Health Systems Migration
hero.subtitle: How to change systems without dying in the attempt

card.author: Zonda Health Team
card.description: Many health institutions in Latin America face a recurring challenge: legacy systems hinder efficient information management. When clinical management systems are updated, providers are changed, or institutions merge, health teams often find themselves with multiple legacy systems, which slows operations and increases the risk of errors.

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.item2: Reliable and standardized data from day one
outcome.item4: Greater operational efficiency and reduced errors

healthSystemsMigration.introduction: Many health institutions in Latin America face a recurring challenge: legacy systems hinder efficient information management. When clinical management systems are updated, providers are changed, or institutions merge, health teams often find themselves with multiple legacy systems, which slows operations and increases the risk of errors. At Zonda Health, we help institutions move faster, reduce costs, and maintain continuity of care, making system migration safe, reliable, and uninterrupted.

problems.title: What did we find?
problems.intro: When analyzing existing systems, we detected several critical problems:
problems.conclusion: These problems affected both operational efficiency and quality of care and required structured intervention.

lackOfStandardization.title: Lack of data standardization
lackOfStandardization.description: Patient and professional records followed inconsistent formats, complicating integration.

duplicateRecords.title: Duplicate records
duplicateRecords.description: Multiple entries for the same patient, generating confusion in treatments and appointments.

clinicalErrors.title: Clinical errors
clinicalErrors.description: Inverted vital signs or impossible values (for example, 0% oxygen saturation, 20°C body temperature).

unlicensedProfessionals.title: Unlicensed professionals
unlicensedProfessionals.description: Making it difficult to comply with regulations and trust the data.

solution.title: Solution: a comprehensive migration approach
solution.subtitle: A migration process was applied in four key phases, designed to ensure security, integrity, and operational continuity:

phase1.title: Strategic planning
phase1.description: We worked with the client's team to evaluate timelines, data structure, and possible constraints, defining a clear and safe plan for migration.

phase2.title: Data cleaning and preparation
phase2.description: We analyzed and cleaned existing records:
phase2.bullet1: We eliminated duplicates and corrected errors.
phase2.bullet2: We standardized patient and professional information.
phase2.bullet3: We detected and adjusted inconsistent or atypical clinical values.
phase2.conclusion: This step ensured that data was reliable and usable from day one in the new system.

phase3.title: Supervised migration
phase3.description: We transferred data securely, ensuring that all clinical and administrative information was available from day one in the new system.

phase4.title: Validation and launch
phase4.description: We finished with final quality controls, confirming that all records were correct and complete, allowing the clinical team to resume their activities without interruptions.

graph.title: Results Visualization
graph.placeholder: [Placeholder for graph - will be added later]

results.title: Results
results.intro: Thanks to Zonda Health's structured approach, the clinic achieved:

reducedWorkload.title: Reduced internal workload
reducedWorkload.description: IT and clinical staff could focus on their tasks while we managed the migration.

reliableData.title: Reliable and standardized data
reliableData.description: All records were validated, eliminating duplicates and critical errors.

continuity.title: Continuity of care
continuity.description: Uninterrupted access to patient clinical history during and after migration.

efficiency.title: Greater operational efficiency
efficiency.description: Integration between systems and elimination of redundancies, improving administrative and clinical management.

reducedErrors.title: Reduced clinical errors
reducedErrors.description: Vital signs and records adjusted to ensure reliable information from day one.

impact.title: Tangible impact
impact.description: Clinical teams could focus on patient care without needing to redo records or correct previous errors.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.paragraph1: This project demonstrates that modernizing health systems is possible without risks or interruptions. With planning, data cleaning, supervised migration, and final validation, health centers can:
conclusion.bullet1: Improve quality of care and patient safety.
conclusion.bullet3: Ensure integrity and reliability of information.
conclusion.paragraph2: Zonda Health transforms migration processes into an opportunity to elevate efficiency, security, and clinical experience, allowing teams to focus on what really matters: caring for people.

hero.title: Migration and Validation of 250,000 Medical Records

card.author: Zonda Health Team
card.description: A health organization faced a critical challenge: its database contained more than 250,000 medical records with inconsistent formats and historical errors, making it difficult to generate reliable information for clinical decision-making. Zonda Health intervened to migrate, transform, and validate all information within our infrastructure.

medicalRecordsMigrationValidation.introduction: A health organization faced a critical challenge: its database contained more than 250,000 medical records with inconsistent formats and historical errors, making it difficult to generate reliable information for clinical decision-making. Zonda Health intervened to migrate, transform, and validate all information within our infrastructure, ensuring that data was reliable, traceable, and compatible with advanced clinical models.

challenges.title: Identified challenges
challenges.intro: During the initial analysis we detected several problems:

lowQuality.title: Low quality and lack of standardization:
lowQuality.description: the original records had varied formats and many incomplete fields.

criticalErrors.title: Critical recording errors:
criticalErrors.description: blood pressure alerts and other inconsistent vital signs that could impact clinical decisions.

crossedData.title: Crossed or inverted data:
crossedData.description: for example, systolic and diastolic values exchanged (60/120 instead of 120/60), generating incorrect clinical interpretations.

analysisRisk.title: Risk of incorrect analysis:
analysisRisk.description: any model or health profile based on these records was compromised by historical errors.

challenges.conclusion: These challenges required a safe, systematic migration process with automated clinical validation to ensure integrity and reliability.

solution.title: Solution: migration, normalization and automated validation
solution.subtitle: We applied a comprehensive process in three main phases:

phase1.title: 1. Data migration
phase1.bullet1: Extraction of 250,000 records from the original database.
phase1.bullet2: Transformation of information to our platform's structured format.
phase1.bullet3: Secure indexing within our API infrastructure, maintaining complete traceability.

phase2.title: 2. Normalization and standardization
phase2.bullet1: We applied automatic processes to clean incomplete or inconsistent records.
phase2.bullet2: We standardized units and formats, ensuring compatibility with clinical models.
phase2.bullet3: We identified historical error patterns, such as crossed blood pressure values, that affected previous analyses.

phase3.title: 3. Automated clinical validation
phase3.bullet1: Our clinical AI executed anonymized health profiles on the entire patient population.
phase3.bullet2: It detected critical alerts and systematic errors.
phase3.bullet3: It automatically corrected and normalized data, ensuring clinical coherence and record quality.

graph.title: Results Visualization
graph.placeholder: [Placeholder for graph - will be added later]

results.title: Results achieved
results.intro: Thanks to this process, the organization achieved:

cleanDatabase.title: Clean and reliable database:
cleanDatabase.description: 250,000 verified, normalized and clinically coherent records.

errorDetection.title: Detection and correction of historical errors:
errorDetection.description: inverted vital sign values, duplicates and incomplete records were adjusted.

completeProfiles.title: Complete and accurate health profiles:
completeProfiles.description: enabling more reliable and well-founded clinical analyses.

fasterDecisions.title: Faster and safer decision-making:
fasterDecisions.description: medical teams now have consistent data to prioritize care and detect risks.

automation.title: Automation and scalability:
automation.description: the process can be repeated in other databases or integrated into continuous clinical data flows.

tangibleImpact.title: Tangible impact:
tangibleImpact.description: the company can now fully trust its clinical information, reducing errors and improving the efficiency of care processes.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.paragraph1: This project demonstrates that migrating large volumes of medical data is possible without compromising integrity or clinical accuracy. With controlled migration, automated normalization and AI validation, health centers and organizations can:
conclusion.bullet1: Ensure reliable and consistent clinical data.
conclusion.bullet2: Improve the accuracy of medical analyses and reports.
conclusion.bullet3: Optimize patient care based on verified information.
conclusion.paragraph2: Zonda Health converts historically problematic databases into useful, secure information ready for clinical decision-making, strengthening care quality and trust in data.

card.author: Zonda Health Team
card.description: In an environment where economic sustainability and care quality must coexist, healthcare organizations face the challenge of balancing medical spending control with the guarantee of efficient and safe care. Zonda Health supported a major company in the sector in implementing a comprehensive analysis and control system, aimed at optimizing medical practice management and preventing operational fraud.

fraudDetectionMedicalPractices.introduction: In an environment where economic sustainability and care quality must coexist, healthcare organizations face the challenge of balancing medical spending control with the guarantee of efficient and safe care. In this scenario, Zonda Health, through its Data & Analytics and Artificial Intelligence verticals, supported a major company in the sector in implementing a comprehensive analysis and control system, aimed at optimizing medical practice management and preventing operational fraud. The project was developed in two complementary stages that transformed the institution's audit and internal control processes.

implementation1.title: Implementation 1: Treatment Typification and Standardization of Consumption Curves by Diagnosis

challenge.title: Challenge
challenge.description: The institution lacked a standardized model that would allow it to compare medical practice consumption among patients with the same diagnosis. This lack of reference created difficulties in detecting deviations, controlling costs, and evaluating the clinical efficiency of treatments. Additionally, information was fragmented across different systems, without an analytical tool that consolidated historical consumption data or allowed monitoring behaviors in real time.

solution.title: Solution
solution.description: Zonda Health developed a statistical model for treatment typification, which allowed tracing historical consumption curves of medical practices by diagnosis. Based on this data, expected consumption standards were defined for each type of practice and pathology, and integrated into real-time control dashboards.
solution.bullet1: Identifying and categorizing historical deviations, distinguishing cases of overconsumption or underutilization.
solution.bullet2: Detecting deviations in real time, enabling early and preventive intervention by the medical audit area.

results.title: Results
results.bullet2: Complete and real-time visibility of consumption curves.
results.bullet3: Reduction in audit time and improvement in early detection of irregularities.
results.bullet4: Better budget control and resource planning.

implementation2.title: Implementation 2: Fraud Detection in Home Care Practices

challenge.title: Challenge
challenge.description: Controlling home care practices represented a critical point for the company. Differences were detected between visits reported by professionals and those actually performed, which generated economic losses and put operational transparency at risk. The lack of traceability and automated validation meant that auditing depended on slow manual processes with high administrative burden.

solution.title: Solution
solution.description: Zonda Health integrated a fraud detection module for home visits into the same platform, combining geographic data, automatic validations, and direct communication with patients.
solution.controls: The system applies two complementary controls:

control1.title: Excess visit control:
control1.description: Detects and automatically discounts visits reported above those authorized or outside the therapeutic plan range.

control2.title: Unperformed visit control:
control2.description: Analyzes the recorded distance between the professional and the patient's home at the time of the visit. If an inconsistency is detected, the system automatically sends a message to the patient, requesting confirmation of the service through Zonda Health's secure messaging channel.

results.title: Results
results.bullet1: Detection and elimination of visits reported but not performed.
results.bullet2: Automatic validation of the traceability of each practice.
results.bullet3: Reduction of financial losses due to improper billing.
results.bullet4: Greater trust and transparency in the relationship between institution, professionals, and patients.

globalImpact.title: Global Impact of the Project
globalImpact.description: The combination of both implementations allowed the company to:
globalImpact.bullet1: Transform its medical control system into a data-based and predictive analytics model.
globalImpact.bullet2: Reduce operational costs and fraud through automation and early detection.
globalImpact.bullet3: Improve the efficiency, traceability, and quality of medical audits.
globalImpact.bullet4: Have a scalable and rapidly integrable infrastructure that does not alter existing processes and adapts to the institutional ERP.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.description: Through the synergy between Data & Analytics and AI, Zonda Health helped this organization evolve towards an intelligent audit model and evidence-based management, in which data becomes knowledge and control becomes a strategic tool to improve the quality, transparency, and efficiency of the healthcare system.

hero.copy: Zonda connects organizations, professionals and patients in one unified clinical intelligence layer without replacing what already exists.

problem.copy: Every organization manages its data in isolation. Professionals jump between platforms that do not talk to each other. Patients move through disconnected points of care.
problem.statement: It is not a coincidence. It is a structural limitation.

privacyPolicy.metaDescription: Learn how Zonda Health collects, uses and protects your personal data.
privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy

sections.title: Data Collection
sections.content: We collect personal information you provide when using our contact form, creating an account or interacting with our platform. This may include your name, email address, phone number, organization name and any messages you send us.

sections.title: How We Use Your Data
sections.content: We use the information we collect to respond to your inquiries, provide and improve our services, send relevant communications about our platform and comply with legal obligations. We do not use your data for purposes unrelated to the services you requested.

sections.title: Data Sharing
sections.content: We do not sell your personal data. We may share information with trusted service providers who assist us in operating our platform, provided they agree to keep your information confidential. We may also disclose information when required by law.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your personal information, including encryption in transit and at rest, access controls and regular security audits. Our infrastructure is designed to meet healthcare data protection requirements.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: You have the right to access, correct or delete your personal data. You may also request a copy of the data we hold about you or ask us to restrict its processing. To exercise any of these rights, contact us at support@zondahealth.com.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: If you have questions about this privacy policy or how we handle your data, please contact us at support@zondahealth.com.

company.copy: Zonda builds digital infrastructure for healthcare organizations in Latin America. We connect systems, organize data and make operations clearer.
company.missionCopy: Zonda developed a set of tools designed to bring specialized technology to Latin American healthcare organizations. A management platform where data, teams, patients and processes are administered through one operating logic.

metrics.label: Reduction in medical errors with AI applied to clinical data
In diagnosis times thanks to automatic alerts and health profiles
Administrative work freed up for healthcare professionals

company.originCopy1: Zonda was born in San Juan, shaped by three generations and more than 25 years of healthcare management experience inside a home care company. From that real operation, one missing tool became clear: a system capable of connecting every area of an organization with multiple providers and broad geographic coverage.
company.originCopy2: Interoperable technology could generate savings of almost 10% of healthcare spending in Latin America. That is the opportunity Zonda is building.

articles.title: Healthtech: an Argentine startup applies AI to generate savings in the healthcare system
Argentine startup applies AI in healthcare to reduce costs

developers.title: Build on Zonda
developers.copy: Open APIs, Personal Access Token authentication, and a platform designed so your technical team can integrate, automate and scale without friction.

features.title: API with Personal Access Token
features.copy: Authenticate with a PAT and start consuming the Zonda API in minutes. Generate your token, make your first request and build from there.

features.title: Model your organization
features.copy: Create and manage multiple organizations from the API. Define structures, assign roles and manage permissions programmatically.

features.title: Move data from anywhere
features.copy: Connect any source: HL7, FHIR, CSV, PDFs or proprietary systems. Zonda normalizes everything into a canonical model ready to use.

professionals.heroTitle: Manage your clinical practice for the AI era.
professionals.heroCopy: One place to manage patients, record progress notes, organize your schedule and administer the organizations you work with.

features.title: Smart scheduling, informed patients.
features.copy: Manage appointments, cancellations and follow-up from a weekly calendar. We notify patients about changes and reminders. Before every encounter, you have the information you need at hand: socioeconomic surveys, status changes and basic checks. You configure it, we run it.

features.title: One entry, all your organizations.
features.copy: Connect the companies you work with and use Zonda as the only place to register patient visits. Swiss Medical, OSDE, PAMI? No problem. Track your finances in real time, all in one place.

features.title: Superintelligent clinical records.
features.copy: Take notes as if you were writing on paper. We handle understanding, organizing and following up on your patients with artificial intelligence.
bullets: Semantic search across the full history

testimonial.quote: I used to use three different tools to manage patients, organize my schedule and record sessions. Now with Zonda I have everything in one place, I organize my day in minutes and focus on my patients.
testimonial.name: Laura García

download.title: Your practice in your pocket.
download.copy: Download Zonda and take your schedule, patients and clinical records wherever you go. Available for iOS and Android.

professionals.faqCopyPrefix: If you do not find what you are looking for, write to us at
professionals.faqCopySuffix: We respond in less than one business day.

faqs.q: Do I need to install anything in my office?
faqs.a: No. Zonda works in the browser and in the mobile app. All you need is internet, even from your phone.
Yes. End-to-end encryption, storage in Argentina and compliance with Law 26.529 on Patient Rights. Your data is yours.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get operational?
faqs.a: 5 minutes. You register, connect your organizations (OSDE, Swiss Medical, PAMI, etc.) and start loading patients.

faqs.q: Can I migrate my patients from another system?
faqs.a: Yes. We import from Excel, CSV or via API from most popular systems in Argentina. We assist with migration at no cost.

faqs.q: What happens if I want to leave?
faqs.a: You can export all your information in standard formats (FHIR, CSV). No lock-in, no fine print.

phone.pamiHelp: Enter your PAMI username and password. They will only be used to authenticate you with PAMI during care flows, never shown on screen or shared with third parties.

organizations.heroTitle: Full visibility over your healthcare operation.

features.title: Ingest data from any source.
features.copy: Medical records, progress notes, appointments, surveys, devices and more. Zonda centralizes all information about your patients and professionals in one place, no matter where it comes from.
bullets: FHIR R4 · HL7v2 · CSV · REST API

features.title: Detect patterns, anomalies and fraud.
features.copy: Interactive dashboards that adapt to what you need to see. Automatic trend identification in clinical data, real-time alerts when something moves out of range, and custom reports by region, unit or professional.
bullets: Reports exportable to PDF, Excel, FHIR

features.title: Clear roles. Organized teams. Multiple entities.
features.copy: Define your organization structure: teams, professionals, patients, locations and permissions. If you operate with more than one legal entity, manage everything from one place without losing traceability.
bullets: Granular permissions by role and location
Full audit trail of who did what and when
Share reports with stakeholders without breaking compliance

organizations.useCasesHeadline: One system, four ways to operate.

useCases.desc: Clinical operations + billing in one panel. Ideal for single entities with multiple specialties.
Multiple services, strict governance, and integration with legacy hospital systems.
Multiple legal entities, multiple sites, one consolidated view for the holding company.
Home care, telemedicine, small payers. You need to connect the administrative and clinical sides.

sections.title: The platform
sections.description: Zonda was designed as integration infrastructure. A secure, scalable and extensible layer that connects systems, processes data with artificial intelligence and builds applications on a unified foundation.

items.label: AI processing
items.text: Integrated models that process, classify and extract structured clinical information from any data source in real time.

items.label: Operational analytics
items.text: Full observability over data flow, transaction volume and the performance of each active integration.

items.label: Platform as a Service
items.text: Open APIs, webhooks and SDKs that allow healthcare organizations to build their own solutions on Zonda infrastructure.

sections.title: Ways to connect
sections.description: Cloud-native architecture with encryption in transit and at rest, granular permission management and multi-tenant isolation. Security is the responsibility of the platform, not the integrator.

items.label: Zonda Connectors
items.text: Native connectors for the most widely used healthcare systems. Declarative configuration, immediate activation and data exchange without custom development.

items.label: REST APIs
items.text: RESTful interface documented with OpenAPI. Standard authentication, intelligent rate limiting and compatibility with any technology stack.

items.label: FileSystems & DataDrop
items.text: Bulk ingestion through secure filesystems and DataDrop. Native support for HL7, FHIR, CSV and arbitrary formats with automatic transformation.

sections.title: Canonical model
sections.description: Zonda consumes data in any format: plain text, PDFs, CSVs, HL7, FHIR, XMLs, and produces a single canonical data model. A structured, normalized output ready to be consumed by any downstream system.

items.label: LOINC
items.text: Automatic normalization of observations and lab results under the LOINC standard. Semantic mapping from proprietary terminology to universal codes.

items.label: ICD-10
items.text: Diagnosis classification and coding under ICD-10. Code extraction from free clinical text through natural language processing.

items.label: Flexible output
items.text: Export the canonical model in the format you need: JSON, FHIR, CSV, HL7 or custom schemas. One input, multiple outputs adapted to each consumer.

sections.title: Security and Identity
sections.description: Zonda models the structure of the healthcare business. It provides unified identity for professionals, patients and members of each organization, with defined roles and granular permissions.

items.label: Inbound OAuth2
items.text: External applications authenticate against Zonda through OAuth 2.0 to operate on platform APIs with scoped tokens and standard flows.

items.label: Outbound OAuth2
items.text: Zonda acts as an OAuth 2.0 client when connecting to third-party systems, delegating authentication without exposing end-user credentials.

items.label: Adaptive integration
items.text: Integration capability at every level of the architecture. From out-of-the-box connectors to programmable extensions on top of the API.

idom.heroTitle: Your whole team connected, from the office to the patient’s home.
idom.heroCopy: Organize visits, coordinate professionals and control every step of home care from one place. No spreadsheets, no calls, no surprises.
idom.flowHeadline: From office to home in four steps.
idom.flowCopy: One platform for the whole journey. No paper, no phone calls.

flow.title: Assignment
flow.desc: From a single screen, the day’s visits are assigned to each professional with the best route and no duplicates.

flow.title: Notice
flow.desc: The professional receives patient data, the address and care instructions on their phone before leaving.

flow.title: Visit
flow.desc: Arrival is confirmed automatically, everything performed is recorded, and the patient signs from the phone.

flow.title: Control
flow.desc: Every visit is logged instantly with location, time and detail. Ready to present to the payer.

office.title: Organize every visit of the day from one screen.
office.copy: Assign professionals by zone, availability and specialty. If someone gets sick or an emergency appears, reassign with one click. Everything is recorded.
bullets: Map or list view to organize the day
Know each professional’s load in real time

field.title: The professional records the visit from the patient’s home.
field.copy: They receive the assigned visit, follow the best route, record what they did by speaking to the phone and get the patient’s signature. All from the app, even without internet.
bullets: Dictate the progress note by voice and AI organizes it
Presence is confirmed automatically by location

audit.title: Audit-ready before the day closes.
audit.copy: Know exactly what was done, where and when. If something does not match, you find out immediately, before it becomes a problem with the payer.
bullets: Automatic record with exact location and time
Report ready for billing and auditing

ui.voiceNote: Patient tolerating diet well. Saturation 96. No pain reported...

idom.faqPrefix: Have another question? Write to us at

faqs.a: Yes. The app stores everything the professional does on the phone and uploads it when signal returns. Information is never lost and work does not stop.

faqs.q: Does it connect with our billing system?
faqs.a: Yes. We integrate with the most commonly used billing systems in Argentina. Our team configures the connection during implementation at no additional cost.

faqs.q: Does it work for any payer?
faqs.a: We work with OSDE, Swiss Medical, PAMI, IOMA and many more formats. If your payer uses a different format, we add it quickly.

faqs.q: Does each person only see what they should?
faqs.a: Yes. Each role has its own view: the professional sees their visits, the coordinator sees their team, and the director sees everything. There is also a record of who did what.
Between 2 and 4 weeks. We start with configuration and connection to your system, then run a test with a small team, and from there expand to the rest.

testimonial.name: Lic. Fernanda Casás
testimonial.quote: We used to argue with the payer at the end of the month over poorly documented visits. Today every visit is recorded before the professional returns to the office. We collect faster and audit with much less friction.

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.description: Find answers to common questions about the Zonda platform and health technology solutions.

dataSafety.question: Is patient data safe with Zonda?
dataSafety.answer: Yes. Zonda follows cybersecurity guidelines, is fully compliant with high security practices both in transit and at rest, with access controls.
